Who is the traditionally attributed author of the Symposium?

The Symposium (會飲篇) is traditionally attributed to Plato. It is one of his most celebrated dialogues, depicting a series of speeches in praise of Eros (love) at a banquet, culminating in Socrates' account of wisdom he received from Diotima. While Socrates appears as a central character in the dialogue, the Symposium was written by Plato — Socrates himself left no written works. The attribution to Plato is considered reliable within classical scholarship.
